patch:
diff --git a/asgiref/server.py b/asgiref/server.py
index 131ea43..7d43f9c 100644
--- a/asgiref/server.py
+++ b/asgiref/server.py
@@ -98,7 +98,7 @@ class StatelessServer:
             self.application_instances[scope_id]["last_used"] = time.time()
             return self.application_instances[scope_id]["input_queue"]
         # See if we need to delete an old one
-        while len(self.application_instances) > self.max_applications:
+        while len(self.application_instances) >= self.max_applications:
             self.delete_oldest_application_instance()
         # Make an instance of the application
         input_queue = asyncio.Queue()
@@ -158,7 +158,6 @@ class StatelessServer:
                     try:
                         del self.application_instances[scope_id]
                     except KeyError:
-                        # Exception handling might have already got here before us. That's fine.
                         pass
 
     async def application_exception(self, exception, application_details):
@@ -170,4 +169,4 @@ class StatelessServer:
             exception,
             "".join(traceback.format_tb(exception.__traceback__)),
             f"  {exception}",
-        )
+        )
\ No newline at end of file
diff --git a/asgiref/timeout.py b/asgiref/timeout.py
index fd5381d..52ba854 100644
--- a/asgiref/timeout.py
+++ b/asgiref/timeout.py
@@ -82,8 +82,6 @@ class timeout:
             return None
 
     def _do_enter(self) -> "timeout":
-        # Support Tornado 5- without timeout
-        # Details: https://github.com/python/asyncio/issues/392
         if self._timeout is None:
             return self
 
@@ -93,14 +91,13 @@ class timeout:
                 "Timeout context manager should be used " "inside a task"
             )
 
-        if self._timeout <= 0:
+        if self._timeout < 0:
             self._loop.call_soon(self._cancel_task)
             return self
 
         self._cancel_at = self._loop.time() + self._timeout
         self._cancel_handler = self._loop.call_at(self._cancel_at, self._cancel_task)
         return self
-
     def _do_exit(self, exc_type: Type[BaseException]) -> None:
         if exc_type is asyncio.CancelledError and self._cancelled:
             self._cancel_handler = None
